mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 343623M as of May 2005 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This constrains equity valuations and forward returns become negative over the next 60 trading days as rate expectations remain elevated and discount rates compress cash flow values.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.0% as of May 2005, near tightening threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with stable or declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates tight labor markets and strong economic momentum. This dual signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved earnings growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133347K and UNRATE at 5.1% as of May 2005
failure_condition: Joint signal (rising PAYEMS AND stable/falling UNRATE) fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal weakening supply-side capacity utilization and potential stagflation risk. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth and inflation expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum fails to produce negative 5-day returns in > 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength. When both rise simultaneously, the mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through sustained GDP growth and corporate profit expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2038K and RSAFS at 343623M as of April-May 2005
failure_condition: Joint signal (rising HOUST AND rising RSAFS) fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 3.5%, creating a real rate compression that signals policy lag. This regime predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate future rate hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 194.1 and FEDFUNDS at 3.0% as of May 2005
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: Policy lag signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% in a single month, signaling labor market deterioration and recession risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% as of May 2005, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: UNRATE > 5.5% fails to produce negative 5-day returns in > 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades and multiple compression.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 2.5% fails to produce negative 60-day returns in > 56% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
